title: Bibliography and citation authoring
summary: A document with one bibliography source (`smith2020`) stored in `customXml/item{N}.xml` under `<b:Sources>`, and a matching inline `<w:sdt>` citation reference in `word/document.xml` carrying a `<w:citation/>` marker and a complex `CITATION` field instruction.

spec.notes: The bibliography namespace is `http://schemas.openxmlformats.org/officeDocument/2006/bibliography` (`b:` prefix). A bibliography part is a customXml data part at `/customXml/item{N}.xml` with a `<b:Sources>` root; each source is a `<b:Source>` child carrying `<b:Tag>`, `<b:SourceType>`, `<b:Title>`, `<b:Year>`, and one or more `<b:Author>` entries. Citation references in the document body are `<w:sdt>` content controls whose `<w:sdtPr>` carries a `<w:citation/>` type marker; `<w:sdtContent>` holds a complex-field instruction of the form ` CITATION <tag> \l <lcid> `. Word tolerates implicit binding by `<b:Tag>` value; explicit `<w:dataBinding>` is not required for the citation to round-trip through Word.

assertions.description: A `<b:Sources>` root element must be present in `/customXml/item1.xml`.
A `<b:Source>` whose `<b:Tag>` text equals `smith2020` must exist.
The `smith2020` source must carry the expected `<b:Title>`.
The `smith2020` source must carry the expected `<b:Year>`.
The sibling `itemProps1.xml` must declare the bibliography schemaRef URI.
A `<w:sdt>` with a `<w:citation/>` marker inside its `<w:sdtPr>` must exist (the citation reference produced by `Paragraph.add_citation_reference`).
The citation SDT must carry a `CITATION <tag>` complex-field instruction referencing `smith2020`.
A `customXml` relationship from `word/document.xml` to the bibliography part must be retained in the output package.

render_assertions.description: Renderers that do not resolve CITATION fields should fall back to the cached result text — python-docx emits `(smith2020)` by default when no explicit `result_text` is supplied.

authoring.notes: This manifest exercises the authoring surface shipped in python-docx 2026.05.7. The generator script builds a fresh document, calls `Document.add_citation` once, wires an inline citation reference into a paragraph, and saves to the machine-fixture output directory.
